name: Reputation Choice
description: Reputation Choice is a story-state mechanic that presents, records, branches, or reacts to player decisions.
design_notes: Reputation Choice should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For narrative mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use reputation choice as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect reputation choice to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels their attention and decisions matter to the story state.

parameters.name: choice_count
parameters.description: Number of options or branches presented to the player.

parameters.name: state_flag_count
parameters.description: Number of flags or facts tracked by the mechanic.

parameters.name: reveal_timing
parameters.description: When consequences or information become visible.

edge_cases: Reputation Choice is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Reputation Choice overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Reputation Choice runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Reputation Choice state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Reputation Choice plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Reputation Choice produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Reputation Choice strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Reputation Choice becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Reputation Choice with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Reputation Choice as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Reputation Choice with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Reputation Choice as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
